title: Java Flexible Constructors
description: Validate arguments before calling super() with a flexible constructor body.
learningGoals: Run validation logic before an explicit super() call (JEP 513, final in Java 25).
Recall that pre-super() code cannot read the current instance under construction.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if this constructor reads this.side before super(side * side)?
choices: Validation that uses only constructor parameters
Calling an instance method on this
completionChecks.explanation: The constructor prologue may validate parameters before super(), but reading the current instance before delegation fails to compile.

completionChecks.prompt: What still has to happen exactly once in a flexible constructor body?
choices: A constructor invocation such as super(...) or this(...)
completionChecks.explanation: Flexible constructor bodies change where validation can appear, not the rule that construction delegates exactly once.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens when new Square(0) is evaluated?
choices: IllegalArgumentException is thrown before the Shape constructor is called
A Square is created with side set to 0
Compilation fails because super(...) is not the first statement
The Shape constructor receives 0 and then side is assigned
completionChecks.explanation: The constructor prologue may reject the parameter before delegation, so the trap is assuming old super-first ordering.
